clusters.title: Window rollover can erase samples recorded for the new window

member_findings.title: Window rollover can erase samples recorded for the new window
member_findings.impact: At a window boundary, the winning thread first publishes the new window start and only then exchanges the shared counters to zero. A concurrent recorder can observe the new start, add its success/failure sample to the current-window counter, and then have that sample wiped by the settling thread's exchange. Under concurrent Set/Get traffic this makes the tracker compute incorrect sample counts and success rates, which can suppress or delay URMA failover even though the data plane is unhealthy.
member_findings.fix: Make rollover atomic with respect to counter updates, for example by protecting windowStartTimeMs_ and the counters with one small mutex, or by moving to per-window counter objects/generation-tagged counters so recorders cannot add to the new generation before the old generation is snapshotted and reset.

clusters.title: Pipelined URMA write failover path is untested

member_findings.title: Pipelined URMA write failover path is untested
member_findings.impact: The new tracker recording after PipelineDataTransferHelper is only exercised when the payload exceeds the URMA buffer segment and uses the pipelined write path. The added ST uses a 512 KiB value, below the default 2 MiB urma_max_write_size_mb threshold, so it covers the single-write branch but would not fail if the pipelined branch stopped recording failures or successes.
member_findings.fix: Add a Set/Put failover test that forces the pipelined UB transfer path, for example with a value larger than the configured URMA max write size, injects a URMA write failure, and asserts the unhealthy window triggers URMA_DATA_PLANE_FAILURE switching.

clusters.title: Disabled failover still performs atomic reset work per URMA result

member_findings.title: Disabled failover still performs atomic reset work per URMA result
member_findings.impact: When operators set urma_failover_success_rate_ratio to 0.0 to disable URMA failover, every Set/Get URMA result still enters RecordUrmaResult and calls ResetWindow, which performs multiple release atomic stores on the foreground data path. On high-QPS clients this makes the documented disabled mode add avoidable cache-line traffic and latency instead of reducing runtime overhead.
member_findings.fix: Make the disabled fast path cheap: clear tracker state only on the transition to disabled, or guard the call sites so RecordUrmaResult is not invoked while the feature is disabled. Keep the per-request path to a simple read/return when the feature is off.

clusters.title: New URMA failover gflag validators lack reject-case tests

member_findings.title: New URMA failover gflag validators lack reject-case tests
member_findings.impact: The PR adds operator-facing validation for the failover success-rate ratio and minimum sample count, including non-finite/out-of-range ratio values and zero sample count. The added tests only set valid values directly or through the client config, so invalid config could be silently accepted or the validator could be disconnected without any regression test failing.
member_findings.fix: Extend the flags tests or client-config hot-update tests to set invalid values such as -0.1, 1.1, NaN/inf for urma_failover_success_rate_ratio and 0 for urma_failover_min_sample_count, then assert SetCommandLineOption/config reload rejects them and preserves the previous valid value.

member_findings.title: Remove checked-in AI conversation transcript
member_findings.impact: This new file is an implementation transcript rather than source, test, or durable repository documentation. Keeping it in the tree creates noisy future diffs and leaves readers with an uncategorized artifact that can drift from the actual code and reviewed design.
member_findings.fix: Drop the .conversations JSONL file from the PR, or move any source-backed rationale that should persist into the relevant design or repo-context documentation.
